channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: West Gate: A Theatrical Depiction of Melbourne’s Deadliest Bridge Collapse
item.description: The Melbourne Theatre Company’s production of 'West Gate' brings to life the 50-year-old tragedy of the West Gate Bridge collapse, which claimed 35 lives. Written by Dennis McIntosh, the play is a fic...

item.title: Flight Operations Disrupted to UAE Amid Iran-US Conflict, Airlines Update Schedules
item.description: Due to the ongoing Iran-US conflict, several Indian airlines including IndiGo and Air India have announced changes to their flight schedules to the United Arab Emirates, especially Dubai. IndiGo has i...

item.title: Filipino Band 1621 Releases Their Latest EP 'Begin'
item.description: Filipino band 1621 has officially released their new extended play (EP) titled 'Begin,' offering fresh tracks to their growing audience. This release showcases the band's evolving sound, blending cont...

item.title: Complete Guide to Applying Online at Mzumbe University for 2026/2027 Intake
item.description: Mzumbe University in Morogoro, Tanzania, is accepting online applications for the 2026/2027 intake for both undergraduate and postgraduate programmes. The university has three campuses: the main campu...

item.title: KZN MEC urges eThekwini to honour Mangosuthu Buthelezi with statue to reflect inclusive liberation history
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal MEC for Co-operative Governance and Traditional Affairs, Thulasizwe Buthelezi, has publicly called on the city of eThekwini and the provincial government to honour the late IFP founder, ...

item.title: Tyler McGregor: Leading Canada’s Para Ice Hockey Team and Advocating for Mental Health
item.description: Tyler McGregor, captain of Canada’s Para ice hockey team, is preparing to lead his team against the United States for gold at the 2026 Paralympics. A native of Forest, Ontario, McGregor has competed i...

item.title: Daily Horoscope Guidance for March 15, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail presents a detailed horoscope for March 15, 2026, offering insights for all zodiac signs. Aries are encouraged to seek help despite their usual independence, as support from friends...

item.title: PDP Factions Plan to Harmonize Conditions to Resolve Internal Crisis
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) dey try put their house in order as factions of Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde and FCT Minister Nyesom Wike dey plan to harmonize conditions to solve di long-standi...

item.title: Delhi NCR experiences light rain and thunderstorms, meteorological department issues 2-day orange alert
item.description: Delhi and the surrounding NCR region experienced light rainfall accompanied by intermittent thunderstorms, causing a temporary relief from the prevailing hot and humid conditions. The India Meteorolog...

item.title: Iran's Strategic Position and Conditions for a Lasting Ceasefire
item.description: Iran occupies a strategically significant position in the Gulf, controlling the northern coast and dominating southern energy resources. Its influence extends through its allies, such as the Houthi fo...

item.title: PCP Warns Against Using Unregistered GLP-1 Diabetes Medications
item.description: The Philippine College of Physicians (PCP) has issued a warning to the public about the increasing use and promotion of compounded GLP-1 receptor agonists, such as semaglutide and tirzepatide, for the...

item.title: Kabarak University 2026 Fee Structure, Courses, and Application Details for Self-Sponsored and KUCCPS Students
item.description: Kabarak University, a private Christian-oriented institution started in 2002 by the late President Daniel Arap Moi, has updated its fee structure for 2026. The fees depend on whether you're self-spons...

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Oil Hub, Escalating Middle East Conflict
item.description: Acha, seems like things are really heating up in the Middle East! The US has bombed Kharg Island, which is, like, super important for Iran’s oil exports – nearly 90% of it passes through there. Presid...

item.title: Haryana Jeweller Arrested in ₹590 Crore IDFC First Bank Fraud Case
item.description: In a major development in Haryana, the State Vigilance and Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) apprehended Rajan Katodia, the owner of Sawan Jewellers, in connection with the ₹590 crore fraud at IDFC First B...

item.title: How Kyle and Jackie O Challenged Australian Radio Standards
item.description: For nearly thirty years, the Kyle and Jackie O breakfast show operated under the guise of rules and regulations that were rarely enforced. Despite the existence of broadcasting standards, a communicat...

item.description: The Technical University of Mombasa (TUM) offers a variety of competitive courses across diploma, degree, and certificate levels aimed at equipping students with practical skills, entrepreneurship kno...

item.title: Renault Reveals Bridger Concept: A Roomy and Tech-Focused Sub-Compact Crossover
item.description: Renault has unveiled the Bridger Concept, a new sub-compact crossover (B-SUV) aimed at the increasingly crowded market segment. It's designed to compete with models like the Nissan Magnite and Suzuki ...

item.title: Power Minister Urges Patience as Nigeria Faces Electricity Shortages
item.description: Power Minister Adebayo Adelabu don call on Nigerians make dem dey patient as dem dey try improve power sector under President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. He talk say small patience go make people see the solid...

item.title: Comelec to decide on Rodante Marcoleta’s campaign spending case this week
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced that it will release its decision on the case involving Senator Rodante Marcoleta’s Statement of Contributions and Expenditures (SOCE) by Wednesday, Mar...

item.title: Trump calls on international allies to send warships to secure the Strait of Hormuz amid rising tensions
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has urged the UK and other nations, including China, France, Japan, and South Korea, to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping route for global oil...
